description: Cylinder size candidates screened for Stage 1C feasibility (see this task's chat report, 'Cylinder/Can 후보 크기 선정' and docs/experiments/so101_stage1c_cylinder_feasibility_summary.md). Screened via V1's own unmodified run_pick_and_place_episode() with a cylinder collision/visual shape substituted through the additive object_shape='cylinder' scene_config override -- no backend behavior change for the existing box/cube default.
screening_method: Each candidate spawned at 5 position groups (center, interior, edge, corner, x_min_corridor; 1 seed each) via V1's unmodified pipeline. Legacy grasp-trigger success was 100% for EVERY candidate tested (0.03m through 0.10m diameter) because the grasp mechanism is a fixed-distance + gripper-angle constraint, not contact-force based (contact_count measured 0 for all candidates, including the already-validated 0.04m cube/box baseline) -- so raw grasp-trigger success could NOT discriminate candidate feasibility on its own; selection was instead anchored to the one magnitude already validated extensively in this project (Stage 1A/1B: 0.04m closing-axis width).

gripper_geometry_notes.gripper_joint_type: revolute (rotating single moving jaw against gripper_link body), NOT a linear parallel-jaw slide -- see third_party/so101_arm/so101_new_calib.urdf's own <joint name="gripper" type="revolute">
gripper_geometry_notes.contact_based_grasp_verification: not meaningful in this simulation as currently modeled -- contact_count between robot and object measured 0 at the grasp moment across all tested cylinder radii (0.015m-0.05m) AND the pre-existing cube/box baseline; grasp success is judged purely by EE-to-object-center distance (GRASP_DISTANCE_THRESHOLD_M=0.04m) and gripper closed-angle threshold (GRASP_GRIPPER_CLOSED_THRESHOLD=0.15 normalized), both shape/size agnostic
gripper_geometry_notes.no_documented_numeric_max_opening_spec: third_party/so101_arm/README.md and SOURCE.md do not state a numeric max-opening/diameter spec for this gripper design

candidates.name: small
candidates.gripper_clearance_note: smaller than the validated 0.04m cube/box width; screened successfully at all 5 position groups
candidates.scene_validity: valid at all 5 position groups tested (center/interior/edge/corner/x_min_corridor)

candidates.name: medium_can
candidates.gripper_clearance_note: identical footprint scale to the already-validated cube (object_footprint_xy=[0.02,0.02]) and rectangular box closing-axis width (BOX_FOOTPRINT_XY[0]*2=0.04m) -- isolates shape as the only new variable, matching this project's own Stage 1B methodology
candidates.scene_validity: valid at all 5 position groups tested (center/interior/edge/corner/x_min_corridor)
candidates.selection_rationale: Reuses the one magnitude already validated across thousands of Stage 1A/1B episodes, minimizing confound risk. Clearly distinguishable from cube/box by SHAPE alone (rotationally symmetric collision geometry), no size difference needed for distinctiveness. Expert-only evaluation (benchmark/evaluate_so101_expert_v2_cylinder.py) confirmed 125/125 = 100% legacy_success AND physical_success across all 5 position groups and 5 scene-yaw values (0/45/90/135/180 degrees, rotational-symmetry check).

candidates.name: near_limit
candidates.gripper_clearance_note: 40% larger diameter than the validated cube/box scale; also screened successfully (legacy criterion) at all 5 position groups, but introduces a second simultaneously-varying factor (size, on top of shape) that was not pursued further this task to keep the feasibility study clean (single-variable-at-a-time, matching Stage 1B's own stated discipline)
candidates.scene_validity: valid at all 5 position groups tested (center/interior/edge/corner/x_min_corridor)

excluded_by_task_scope: on-its-side or tilted cylinder (upright only, per this task's own absolute scope)
real beverage-can literal dimensions (task explicitly deprioritizes exact real-world replication in favor of sim-stable grasp size)
